Crypto.base64Encode.doc: Encodes a string into Base64 format.

Crypto.sha1.doc: Computes the SHA-1 hash of the given string and returns it as a hex string.

Crypto.sha1Base64.doc: Computes the SHA-1 hash of the given string and returns it as a Base64 string.

File.exists.doc: Checks if a file exists at the given path.

File.open.doc: Opens a file with the specified mode.

params.label: mode: String
params.doc: Mode to open the file in (e.g. 'r', 'w').

File.read.doc: Reads the entire contents of the opened file.

File.remove.doc: Removes the file at the specified path.

File.write.doc: Writes data to the opened file.

Http.get.doc: Performs an HTTP GET request and returns the response body.

Http.post.doc: Performs an HTTP POST request and returns the response body.

Json.parse.doc: Parses a JSON string into a Trypillia object.

Json.stringify.doc: Converts a Trypillia object into a JSON string.

List.filter.doc: Creates a new list with elements that pass the test implemented by the callback.

List.forEach.doc: Executes a provided function once for each list element.

params.label: callback: Function
params.doc: Function to execute on each element.

List.insert.doc: Inserts an item into the list at the specified index.

List.join.doc: Joins all elements of a list into a string.

List.length.doc: Returns the number of elements in the list.

List.map.doc: Creates a new list populated with the results of calling the provided function on every element.

List.pop.doc: Removes the last element from a list and returns it.

List.push.doc: Adds one element to the end of an list.

List.remove.doc: Removes the element at the specified index.

Map.has.doc: Returns true if the map contains the specified key.

Map.keys.doc: Returns an array of all keys in the map.

Map.remove.doc: Removes the specified key from the map and returns its value.

Map.set.doc: Sets the value for the key in the map object.

params.label: key: Any
params.doc: The key of the element to add or update.

params.label: value: Any
params.doc: The value of the element to add or update.

Map.values.doc: Returns an array of all values in the map.

Math.cos.doc: Returns the cosine of the given angle.

Math.random.doc: Returns a random float between 0.0 and 1.0.

Math.sin.doc: Returns the sine of the given angle.

OS.args.doc: Returns an array containing the command-line arguments passed when the process was launched.

OS.exit.doc: Terminates the current process with the specified exit code.

OS.getEnv.doc: Retrieves the value of an environment variable.

params.label: name: String
params.doc: The name of the environment variable.

Random.choice.doc: Returns a random element from the non-empty list.

Random.float.doc: Returns a random float between 0.0 and 1.0.

Random.int.doc: Returns a random integer between min and max inclusive.

Regex.match.doc: Matches a regex pattern against a text and returns the groups.

Regex.replace.signature: Regex.replace(pattern: String, text: String, replacement: String) -> String
Regex.replace.doc: Replaces occurrences of a regex pattern in a text.

Regex.test.doc: Tests if a regex pattern matches a text.

Result.isErr.doc: Returns true if the Result is an error.

Result.isOk.doc: Returns true if the Result is ok.

Result.unwrap.doc: Unwraps the value if ok, otherwise panics.

Result.unwrapErr.doc: Unwraps the error if it is an error, otherwise panics.

Socket.accept.doc: Accepts an incoming connection on a listening socket.

Socket.connect.doc: Connects to a remote host and port.

Socket.listen.doc: Listens for incoming connections on a port.

String.endsWith.doc: Determines whether a string ends with the characters of a specified string.

params.label: search: String
params.doc: The characters to be searched for at the end of this string.

String.includes.doc: Determines whether the string includes a certain value.

String.indexOf.doc: Returns the index of the first occurrence of the specified value.

String.length.doc: Returns the number of characters in the string.

String.replace.doc: Returns a new string with some or all matches of a pattern replaced by a replacement.

String.split.doc: Splits a string into an array of substrings.

params.label: separator: String
params.doc: The pattern describing where each split should occur.

String.startsWith.doc: Determines whether a string begins with the characters of a specified string.

params.label: search: String
params.doc: The characters to be searched for at the start of this string.

String.substring.doc: Returns the part of the string between the start and end indexes.

params.label: start: Int
params.doc: The index of the first character to include.

params.label: end: Int
params.doc: The index of the first character to exclude.

String.toNumber.doc: Parses a string and returns a floating point number.

String.trim.doc: Removes whitespace from both ends of a string.

Terminal.color.doc: Sets the console text color using standard ANSI color names (e.g. 'red', 'green', 'blue', 'gray').

Terminal.disableRawMode.doc: Restores the terminal to its original mode.

Terminal.enableRawMode.doc: Enables raw mode for the terminal (disables echoing and line buffering).

Terminal.readChar.doc: Reads a single character from the console.

Terminal.reset.doc: Resets the console text color to the default.

Terminal.setCursor.doc: Sets the cursor to a specific row and column.

Terminal.write.doc: Writes a string directly to the console output without appending a newline.

Time.format.doc: Formats a Unix timestamp to a string.

Time.now.doc: Returns the current time in milliseconds since the Unix Epoch.

Time.sleep.doc: Pauses the execution for the specified number of milliseconds.

params.label: ms: Int
params.doc: The time to sleep in milliseconds.

WeakRef.init.doc: Initializes a weak reference to an object.

WeakRef.lock.doc: Attempts to lock and return the strongly-referenced object, or nil if collected.

WebSocket.recv.doc: Receives a frame from the WebSocket.

WebSocketServer.create.doc: Creates a WebSocket server on the given port.

Worker.create.doc: Creates a background worker running the given script.

Worker.receive.doc: Receives a message from the worker.

Worker.selfReceive.doc: Receives a message sent to the worker from the main thread.

Worker.selfSend.doc: Sends a message from the worker back to the main thread.

Worker.send.doc: Sends a message to the background worker.

input.doc: Reads a line of input from the console.

params.label: prompt: String
params.doc: The prompt displayed to the user.

print.doc: Prints a message to the console.

params.label: message: Any
params.doc: The object or text to print.
